title: Scalability Bottlenecks
summary: The architecture contains centralized or blocking components that limit growth.
inspection_goal: Find structurally central resources or synchronous chains likely to constrain throughput, latency, or growth.
primary_signals: Very high betweenness or centrality around shared resources
Single shared databases or services on many paths
inspection_questions: Which nodes sit on many critical dependency paths?
Are there centralized stores or services that many components depend on?
Where could synchronous chains amplify latency?
What components are likely points of contention at scale?
evidence_to_collect: Dependency concentration around databases or core services
recommendation_patterns: Introduce queues, caching, or replication where appropriate
Reduce dependence on shared bottleneck components
Split or shard overloaded central services or stores

severity_guidance.high: A few centralized components dominate critical paths and growth limits.
severity_guidance.medium: Bottlenecks are emerging but not yet dominant.
severity_guidance.low: Scalability risks are localized and structurally bounded.
